edges.source: DNA Damage Response and Premature Chromosome Condensation
Centrosomal Continuum with Broader Cortical Malformations

nodes.description: Several MCPH centrosome/centriole/spindle genes do not produce isolated microcephaly with a purely simplified gyral pattern but instead shade into the broader malformations of cortical development, blurring the nosological boundary between primary microcephaly and migration/organization disorders. WDR62 (MCPH2) is already associated with pachygyria, polymicrogyria, and corpus callosum hypoplasia in this entry. NDE1, which like ASPM and WDR62 localizes to the centrosome and mitotic spindle poles, causes extreme microcephaly with a grossly simplified gyral structure termed microlissencephaly. KATNB1, the regulatory subunit of the microtubule-severing enzyme katanin, causes complex cerebral malformations by disrupting asymmetrically dividing neural progenitors. RTTN, a centrosome- and basal-body-associated protein, causes bilateral diffuse polymicrogyria. The shared feature is that the same apical-progenitor centrosomal machinery, when disrupted, can perturb both the proliferative expansion of progenitors and the downstream organization/migration of their progeny, producing combined proliferation-and-organization phenotypes along a mechanistic continuum. Whether these genes are best modeled as MCPH subtypes, related entries, or separate disease entries is an open lumping/splitting question (see the knowledge-gap discussion).
nodes.id: Centrosomal Continuum with Broader Cortical Malformations

evidence.reference_title: Human mutations in NDE1 cause extreme microcephaly with lissencephaly [corrected].
evidence.snippet: we report two families with extreme microcephaly and grossly simplified cortical gyral structure, a condition referred to as microlissencephaly, and show that they carry homozygous frameshift mutations in NDE1, which encodes a multidomain protein that localizes to the centrosome and mitotic spindle poles.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in KATNB1 cause complex cerebral malformations by disrupting asymmetrically dividing neural progenitors.
evidence.snippet: we provide insight into the mechanisms by which KATNB1 mutations cause human cerebral cortical malformations, demonstrating its fundamental role during brain development.

evidence.reference_title: RTTN mutations link primary cilia function to organization of the human cerebral cortex.
evidence.snippet: We have identified autosomal-recessive mutations in RTTN, encoding Rotatin, in individuals with bilateral diffuse polymicrogyria from two separate families.

nodes.description: The MCPH genes encode proteins that localize to the centrosome, centriole, pericentriolar matrix, or mitotic spindle pole. Loss-of-function variants impair centriole biogenesis (CENPJ), pericentriolar microtubule nucleation (CDK5RAP2), spindle pole organization (WDR62, ASPM), or coupling of spindle dynamics to the DNA-damage response (MCPH1). The shared cellular consequence is mis-oriented or unstable mitotic spindles in apical neural progenitors of the developing neuroepithelium, with perturbed centrosomal microtubule nucleation and spindle pole organization.

evidence.snippet: Many of the causative genes for MCPH encode centrosomal proteins involved in centriole biogenesis.

evidence.reference_title: Molecular genetics of human primary microcephaly: an overview.
evidence.snippet: MCPH gene mutations may lead to the disease phenotype due to a disturbed mitotic spindle orientation, premature chromosomal condensation, signalling response as a result of damaged DNA, microtubule dynamics, transcriptional control or a few other hidden centrosomal mechanisms that can regulate the number of neurons produced by neuronal precursor cells.

nodes.description: A distinct upstream mechanism unique to MCPH1 (microcephalin), in which the BRCT-domain protein microcephalin is required for the DNA-damage response and coordination of mitotic entry with chromosome condensation. Loss of MCPH1 function causes a defective DNA-damage signalling response and premature condensation of chromosomes prior to nuclear envelope breakdown, producing the characteristic prophase-like cells observed on routine karyotype. The disrupted DNA-damage response and uncoupled condensation timing in apical neural progenitors converges on the same downstream depletion of the progenitor pool.
nodes.id: DNA Damage Response and Premature Chromosome Condensation

evidence.reference_title: Molecular genetics of human primary microcephaly: an overview.
evidence.snippet: premature chromosomal condensation, signalling response as a result of damaged DNA

nodes.description: Centrosomal and spindle defects in apical neural progenitors cause a premature shift from symmetric proliferative divisions (which expand the progenitor pool) to asymmetric neurogenic divisions, together with increased apoptosis of progenitors. The founder pool of proliferating progenitors in the ventricular and subventricular zones is therefore depleted during fetal corticogenesis. The result is a reduced final complement of cortical neurons and a proportionally small cerebral cortex with simplified gyration but preserved laminar architecture.

evidence.reference_title: Autosomal recessive primary microcephaly (MCPH): a review of clinical, molecular, and evolutionary findings.
evidence.snippet: Present data suggest that MCPH is the consequence of deficient neurogenesis within the neurogenic epithelium.

evidence.reference_title: ASPM is a major determinant of cerebral cortical size.
evidence.snippet: brain size is controlled in part through modulation of mitotic spindle activity in neuronal progenitor cells
